semanticCore: Likeness, resemblance, exemplar (parable), or standing erect
primaryMeaning: To resemble, or represent as a likeness
quranicRelevance: Crucial for: parables or similitudes (Mathal / Amthal) used in the Quran to convey spiritual truths, the best exemplar or paradigm (al-Mathal al-A'la), and the state of being similar (Mithl). It also refers to standing erect or presenting a likeness.

keyDerivations.meaning: To represent, exemplify, or make a likeness
To stand erect, or be prominent
To assume a likeness, or appear in the shape of ('fatamaththala laha basharan sawiyya')

figurativeOrRareUsages: الطَّرِيقَةُ المُثْلَى (20:104): The most exemplary or best path/state.
مُثَّلَ (Form II): To mutilate a body (though in Quran it is mostly about parables and representation).
discardedNoise: Lexical descriptions of physical mutilation (Muthlah) or physical standing.
summaryEn: The root relates to likeness and representation, playing a vital role in the Quranic method of teaching through parables (Amthal) and comparisons (Mithl).
summaryAr: يتعلق هذا الجذر بالمماثلة والتمثيل، ويلعب دوراً حيوياً في الأسلوب القرآني في التعليم عبر ضرب الأمثال (أمثال) والمقايسات (مثل).
rawText:
أَمْثَلَهُ 4 He set it up: from مَثَلَ “ he stood erect. ” === He set up a butt or mark: see an ex. voce غَرَضٌ. اِمْتَثَلَ أَمْرَهُ 8 He followed his command, order, bidding, or injunction; did like as he commanded, ordered, &c.; (Mgh;) he obeyed his command, order, &c. (Msb.) تَمَاثَلَ 6 He became nearly in a sound, or healthy, state; or near to convalescence: (K:) or he became more like the sound, or healthy, than the unsound, or unhealthy, who is suffering from a chronic and pervading disease; (TA;) or so تماثل لِلْبُرْءِ. (M.) Said also of a wound: (T, S in art. دمل:) and of a disease; like أَشْكَلَ. (TA, art. شكل.) === تَمَاثَلَا i. q. تَشَابَهَا. (M, K in art. سوى.) تَمَاثِيلُ , in the following hemistich of Ibn-Ahmar,

تَمَاثِيلُ قِرْطَاسٍ عَلَى هَبْهَبِيَّةٍ

signifies كُتُبٌ يَكْتُبُونَهَا. (L, in TA, voce هَبْهَبِىٌّ, as signifying a “ light, or active, ” camel.) تَمَثَّلَ بِكَذَا 5 [He affected to be like, or imitated, such a thing;] i. q. تَشَبَّهَ بِهِ. (TA, art. شبه.) === تَمَثَّلَ البَيْتَ and [more commonly] بِالبَيْتِ He used, or applied, the verse as a proverb, or proverbially. (MA.) === See تَشَبَّهَ. جَوْزُ مَاثِلٍ : see جَوْزٌ. مَاثَلَهُ 3 i. q. شَابَهَهُ. (TA.) مَثَلٌ i. q. صِفَةٌ [as meaning A description, condition, state, case, &c.]; (S, K, &c.;) or وَصْفٌ [meaning the same]: (Msb:) or this is a mistake: (Mbr, AAF, TA:) or it may be a tropical signification: (MF, TA:) for in the language of the Arabs it means a description by way of comparison: (AAF, TA:) you say مثل زيد مثل فلان [The description of Zeyd, by way of comparison, or the condition, &c., is that of such a one]: it is from المِثاَلُ and الحَذْوُ: (Mbr, TA:) it is metaphorically applied to a condition, state, or case, that is important, strange, or wonderful. (Ksh, Bd in ii. 16.) The phrase here given is more literally, and better, rendered, The similitude of Zeyd is the similitude, or is that, of such a one; for a similitude is a description by way of comparison. === You say also, جَعَلَهُ مَثَلًا لِكَذَا [He made it (an expression or the like) to be descriptive, by way of comparison, of such a thing]. (TA passim.) [And مَثَلٌ لِكَذَا means An expression denoting, by way of similitude, such a thing.] === عَلَى المَثَلِ As indicative of resemblance to something. === See بَدَلٌ. مَثَلَ 1 aor. ﹹ, inf. n. مُثُولٌ; (S, M, K, &c.;) and مَثُلَ; (M, K;) He stood erect; (S, M, K, &c.;) بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ before him. (S, &c.) === مَثَلَ بِهِ, inf. n. مُثْلَةٌ, He mutilated him; castrated him; namely, a sheep or goat. (TA in art. دجن, from a trad.) مَثَّلَ 2 : see a verse of Kutheiyir in art. رود, conj. 4. === مَثَّلَهُ: see شَبَّهَهُ. مِثَالٌ Quality, made, manner, fashion, and form; (Msb;) a model according to which another thing is made or proportioned; a pattern, (مِقْدَارٌ) by which a thing is measured, proportioned, or cut out: (T:) an example of a class of words, of a rule, &c. === مِنْ غَيْرِ سَبْقِ مِثاَلٍ [Without there having been any precedent]. (Msb in art. قرح, &c.) === [A bed:] بَناَتُ المِثَالِ The daughters of the bed; meaning women. (T in art. بنى.) مِثْلٌ A like; a similar person or thing; match; fellow; an analogue. (K, &c.) See نِدٌّ and voce بَدَلٌ. === A likeness, resemblance, or semblance; see شَبَهٌ. === An equivalent; a requital. === مِثْلَ, used as a denotative of state, means Like. Ex. مَرَّ مِثْلَ البَرْقِ He passed like the lightning. See an ex. in the Kur li. 23; and another, from Sakhr-el-Gheí, voce فَرْضٌ.
